description: Title+surname pair 'Master Orin' introduced ungrounded. Neither 'Orin' nor 'Master Orin' appears in any grounded source. Expected: FAIL.

task.prose: Cassel crossed the archive threshold and stopped. A tall figure stood at the far lectern, leafing through a bound register with unhurried care. 'You are late,' said Master Orin without looking up. 'The review was scheduled for the third bell.' Cassel set her satchel down and said nothing.

writer_request_meta.beatDescription: Cassel arrives at the Civic Archive and encounters an unknown senior examiner who reprimands her for being late to a scheduled review.

gold.notes: 'Master Orin' introduces a named character ('Orin') not present in any grounded source. The title 'Master' alone does not ground the surname 'Orin'. Expected FAIL.

description: Title+surname pair 'Mistress Ilara' introduced ungrounded. Neither 'Ilara' nor any alias appears in grounded context. Expected: FAIL.

task.prose: The letter bore a wax seal Cassel did not recognize — a spiralling compass rose — and was signed in a cramped hand: Mistress Ilara, Deputy Assessor of the Third Tier. Cassel read it twice, then folded it back into the envelope.

writer_request_meta.beatDescription: Cassel receives a sealed letter from an unknown official and must decide how to respond.

gold.notes: 'Mistress Ilara' introduces a named character ('Ilara') not present in any grounded source. 'Third Tier' is a brief-derived location term, not a character. Expected FAIL.

description: Title+surname pair 'Captain Kessrin' introduced ungrounded. Neither 'Kessrin' nor any alias appears in grounded context. Expected: FAIL.

task.prose: Three guards in the livery of the Watch stood at the foot of the steps, and behind them Captain Kessrin in plain grey, no insignia, no rank-band on her sleeve. She had the look of someone who had not been asked to come. 'The Arbiter will see you now,' she said.

writer_request_meta.beatDescription: Cassel is escorted to the Arbiter's chambers by Watch guards and an unnamed senior officer.

gold.notes: 'Captain Kessrin' introduces a named character ('Kessrin') not present in any grounded source. The Arbiter and the Watch are grounded but 'Kessrin' is not. Expected FAIL.

description: Title+surname 'Master Vael' where 'Vael' is in beat-characters and beat-entities grounded source. Expected: PASS.

task.prose: Cassel knocked twice and waited. The door opened on Master Vael — older than she'd expected, his robes slightly too formal for the hour, a stack of folded documents under one arm. He stepped aside to let her in without ceremony.

writer_request_meta.beatDescription: Cassel visits Master Vael, the senior archivist, to collect documents she needs for the audit.

derived_prior_beat: Vael had prepared the documents the night before

gold.notes: 'Vael' is grounded in beat-characters, from_brief, derived_outline_fact, and derived_prior_beat. 'Master Vael' passes via the title+grounded-surname rule. Expected PASS.

description: Named institution 'Office of Structural Integrity' introduced ungrounded. Not present in any grounded source. Expected: FAIL.

task.prose: The order had come from the Office of Structural Integrity — a body Cassel had never had cause to deal with before. It cited three subsections of the charter, each more impenetrable than the last, and concluded with a direction to appear by the end of the week.

writer_request_meta.beatDescription: Cassel receives an official order from an institution she doesn't recognize, requiring her to appear before it.

gold.notes: 'Office of Structural Integrity' is a named institution not present in any grounded source. Expected FAIL.

description: Named institution 'Bureau of Civic Truth' introduced ungrounded. Not present in any grounded source. Expected: FAIL.

task.prose: The Bureau of Civic Truth had not existed, in any formal sense, when Cassel had joined the archive. She knew this because she had been the one to index its foundational charter — a slim document, unremarkable at the time, now apparently the source of considerable difficulty.

writer_request_meta.beatDescription: Cassel recalls a newly-powerful institution she once indexed, realizing it now threatens her work.

gold.notes: 'Bureau of Civic Truth' is a named institution not present in any grounded source. Expected FAIL.

description: Named institution 'Council of Forty-Seven Tongues' introduced ungrounded. Not present in any grounded source. Expected: FAIL.

task.prose: The grievance had been formally lodged with the Council of Forty-Seven Tongues, which meant it was no longer a local matter. Cassel set the notification aside and tried to think of who had enough reach to escalate that quickly.

writer_request_meta.beatDescription: Cassel learns a grievance against her has been escalated to a powerful governing council, expanding the stakes.

gold.notes: 'Council of Forty-Seven Tongues' is a named institution not present in any grounded source. Expected FAIL.

description: Named institution 'Council of Sigils' where it appears in the bible. Expected: PASS.

task.prose: The Council of Sigils had issued its ruling the previous evening, and by morning the Civic Archive had three new interdicts posted at every entrance. Cassel read them with the careful attention of someone who knew that ignorance of a ruling was never an accepted defence.

writer_request_meta.beatDescription: Cassel reads new interdicts issued by the Council of Sigils and assesses their implications for her work.

gold.notes: 'Council of Sigils' appears in the bible and from_brief. Expected PASS.

description: Named place 'Vale of Whispers' introduced ungrounded. Not present in any grounded source. Expected: FAIL.

task.prose: The bond paper listed the property as held in the Vale of Whispers, a district Cassel had never encountered in any prior record. She pulled three index drawers before conceding that the name did not appear in the canonical register.

writer_request_meta.beatDescription: Cassel encounters a property record referencing an unknown district and fails to find it in the archive.

gold.notes: 'Vale of Whispers' is a named place not present in any grounded source. Expected FAIL.

description: Named place 'Crown of Hyran' introduced ungrounded. Not present in any grounded source. Expected: FAIL.

task.prose: The Envoy had come from the Crown of Hyran — a realm Cassel understood only in the abstract, its administrative conventions apparently incompatible with anything in the local charter. The translation alone had taken two days.

writer_request_meta.beatDescription: Cassel processes documents from a foreign realm whose administrative conventions conflict with local ones.

gold.notes: 'Crown of Hyran' is a named realm not present in any grounded source. Expected FAIL.

description: Named place 'the Suncrest Reach' introduced ungrounded. Not present in any grounded source. Expected: FAIL.

task.prose: All three of the disputed claims traced back to land in the Suncrest Reach — a coastal holding that was either an independent protectorate or a dependency of the city, depending on which generation of the charter you preferred to cite.

writer_request_meta.beatDescription: Cassel discovers all disputed land claims originate from the same unresolved coastal territory.

gold.notes: 'the Suncrest Reach' is a named place/region not present in any grounded source. Expected FAIL.

description: Named place 'the Greenmarch' where it appears in the bible. Expected: PASS.

task.prose: The holding in the Greenmarch had been contested for decades — a fact Cassel could verify simply by counting the index cards in the corresponding drawer, which threatened to overflow its slot entirely. She pulled the oldest and began reading.

writer_request_meta.beatDescription: Cassel investigates a long-disputed land holding in the Greenmarch.

gold.notes: 'the Greenmarch' appears in the bible and from_brief. Expected PASS.

description: Named artifact 'the Riverstone Chord' introduced ungrounded. Not present in any grounded source. Expected: FAIL.

task.prose: The bequest enumerated a handful of personal effects and, at the bottom, a single line about the Riverstone Chord — no description, no provenance, just the name and a note that it was to be transferred to the Archive's protective holding upon death.

writer_request_meta.beatDescription: Cassel reads a will that includes a mysterious artifact bequeathed to the archive.

gold.notes: 'the Riverstone Chord' is a named artifact not present in any grounded source. Expected FAIL.

description: Named artifact 'the Iron Veyl' introduced ungrounded. Not present in any grounded source. Expected: FAIL.

task.prose: Maret set the parcel on the table without ceremony. Inside, wrapped in oilcloth, was the Iron Veyl — smaller than Cassel had imagined, its surface dull in a way that seemed to absorb light rather than simply fail to reflect it.

writer_request_meta.beatDescription: Cassel is shown the Iron Veyl, a significant artifact, by Maret.

gold.notes: 'the Iron Veyl' is a named artifact not present in any grounded source. Expected FAIL.

description: Named artifact 'the Sigil of Eight' introduced ungrounded. Not present in any grounded source. Expected: FAIL.

task.prose: The argument hinged on whether possession of the Sigil of Eight constituted legal authority or merely ceremonial precedent — a distinction, Cassel reflected, that the original drafters had either not anticipated or had deliberately left ambiguous.

writer_request_meta.beatDescription: Cassel works through a legal argument about whether a particular artifact confers authority.

gold.notes: 'the Sigil of Eight' is a named artifact not present in any grounded source. Expected FAIL.

description: Named artifact 'the Master Ledger' where it appears in beat-entities grounded source. Expected: PASS.

task.prose: Cassel carried the Master Ledger to the reading alcove and set it open to the year in question. The binding was stiff — the book had not been consulted in some time — and several of the inner pages had begun to bleed ink at their margins.

writer_request_meta.beatDescription: Cassel retrieves the Master Ledger, the authoritative record of civic transactions, to research a disputed entry.

derived_prior_beat: Cassel had been told to check the Master Ledger

description: Named historical event 'the Three Days' War' introduced ungrounded. Not present in any grounded source. Expected: FAIL.

task.prose: The precedent dated to the Three Days' War — a period Cassel knew only in outline, its records largely destroyed and the remainder sealed under a continuing interdict that the Archive had never successfully challenged.

writer_request_meta.beatDescription: Cassel traces a legal precedent back to a historical conflict whose records are incomplete.

gold.notes: 'the Three Days' War' is a named historical event not present in any grounded source. Expected FAIL.

description: Named historical event 'the Quiet Reckoning' introduced ungrounded. Not present in any grounded source. Expected: FAIL.

task.prose: What the senior clerks called the Quiet Reckoning had lasted eighteen months and produced no formal documentation — all decisions made in private sessions whose minutes had been ordered destroyed within a fortnight. Cassel was the first person in a generation to ask for them anyway.

writer_request_meta.beatDescription: Cassel investigates a period of historical administrative purges whose records were deliberately destroyed.

gold.notes: 'the Quiet Reckoning' is a named historical event not present in any grounded source. Expected FAIL.

description: Named historical event 'the Year of Fallen Axes' introduced ungrounded. Not present in any grounded source. Expected: FAIL.

task.prose: The grant predated the Year of Fallen Axes by less than a decade — close enough, argued the petitioner's representative, that it ought to be subject to the subsequent reclassification. Cassel noted the argument and wrote nothing in the margin.

writer_request_meta.beatDescription: Cassel reviews a contested property grant and hears an argument that hinges on a historical administrative reclassification.

description: Named historical event 'the Great Auditing' where it appears in the bible. Expected: PASS.

task.prose: The reform had been enacted in the aftermath of the Great Auditing — everyone who worked in the civic records system understood that, even if they rarely said it aloud. Cassel pulled the relevant index and began counting backwards through the years.

writer_request_meta.beatDescription: Cassel researches a civic reform and traces it to the Great Auditing, the defining accountability crisis of the prior generation.

task.prose: Word had reached her that morning: the Quiet Concord had objected to the review. Not formally — they never did anything formally — but the objection was understood by everyone who needed to understand it.

description: Plural faction 'the Veiled Eight' introduced ungrounded. Not present in any grounded source. Expected: FAIL.

task.prose: The Veiled Eight had not been an official body for sixty years — dissolved by charter after the scandals of the reform era — but their successors still met, and everyone in the civic system knew where and when, even if no one wrote it down.

writer_request_meta.beatDescription: Cassel reflects on a dissolved faction whose informal descendants still exercise hidden influence.

gold.notes: 'the Veiled Eight' is a named faction not present in any grounded source. Expected FAIL.

description: Plural faction 'the Auditors' where it appears in the bible. Expected: PASS.

task.prose: The Auditors had not been seen in the Archive for three years, which meant either the review cycle had ended or they had learned to arrive without announcing themselves. Cassel checked the register for the previous quarter and found no entries.

writer_request_meta.beatDescription: Cassel notes the absence of the Auditors from recent archive logs and tries to determine whether the review cycle has ended.

task.prose: Cassel had spent the morning on the reconciliation report — a tedious exercise in cross-referencing two ledgers that had been maintained by different clerks using incompatible notation systems. By noon she had found four discrepancies and documented none of them definitively.

task.prose: She brought the query to the master archivist, who was, as usual, already aware of it. He had pulled the relevant box file before she finished her first sentence and set it on the counter with the patient air of someone who had answered this question before.
